attacking_style: Not an attacker. Elite leadout specialist operating in final 3km, positioning sprinter optimally through technical corners and exploiting wind conditions for aerodynamic advantage.
team_tactics: Functions as final pilot in Quick-Step leadout train, typically behind Mørkøv. Executes high-risk tactical positioning (left-side in crosswinds) and provides massive final acceleration in last 400-800m before peeling off.
terrain_preference: Excels in technical finales with corners and crosswind opportunities where positioning IQ matters. Thrives when able to exploit wind angles for draft advantage in final 2-3km.
vulnerability: Train effectiveness depends on maintaining sprinter's wheel through technical sections (Concorde corner failure). Headwind conditions force reactive mode, shutting down attacks rather than proactive positioning.
key_patterns: Exploits crosswind angles for aerodynamic advantage, positioning left-side when right-crosswind present (2 observations).
Executes final leadout pull inside 800m with elite acceleration, positioning sprinter optimally (3 observations).
Shows defensive capabilities closing late attacks sub-400m in headwinds, disrupting rival trains (2 observations).
High tactical awareness under pressure, willing to take no-brakes risks through corners (2 observations).
Works as final link in Quick-Step train after Mørkøv (2 observations).
form_trajectory: neutral: Continued elite leadout execution 2017-2019 but train coordination failures (Concorde corner) suggest team cohesion issues rather than individual decline
